Examples

  • Gallery

treemap

treemap partitions a unit square into rectangles sized by a value column, using a squarified tiling (boxes kept as close to square as possible). It emits x, y, w, h for each row, all normalized to [0, 1] — the frame affine places them into pixels. There is no trained domain: the boxes tile relative value and carry no data-space meaning, so nothing is tickable.

It is dual-substrate: JS runs d3-hierarchy's squarify; SQL runs the same squarify state machine as one recursive DuckDB query. Both are deterministic, so the two substrates agree up to float error (equal weights break ties by input order on both sides).

const revenue = db.table('orders').rollup(
  db.table('products'), { left: 'product_id', right: 'id' },
  'category', { amount: sum({ sql: 'qty * price' }) });
const v = canvas(640, 460);
v.rect(revenue, {
  ...treemap('amount').pick('x', 'y', 'w', 'h'),
  fill: ordinal('category'),
  stroke: '#fff',
});
return v;

One rectangle per category, area proportional to revenue, squarified to fill the canvas. The measure is modelled inline: rollup joins orders→products and sums qty*price per category.

Options

treemap(value) takes a single numeric input and produces the full box (all four channels). Negative values clamp to 0. In a template it tiles per cell by default — each panel is its own unit square (perCell: true).

Input Type Meaning
value double the measure whose magnitude sets each tile's area

.pick() outputs:

Output Space Meaning
x relative coord x tile left edge
y relative coord y tile top edge
w relative len x tile width
h relative len y tile height

Bind by spreading the whole box: ...treemap('amount').pick('x', 'y', 'w', 'h'). No options bag; padding is 0 and rounding is off (crisp normalized coordinates).

Examples

Revenue by category, the canonical treemap (as above):

const revenue = db.table('orders').rollup(
  db.table('products'), { left: 'product_id', right: 'id' },
  'category', { amount: sum({ sql: 'qty * price' }) });
const v = canvas(640, 460);
v.rect(revenue, { ...treemap('amount').pick('x', 'y', 'w', 'h'),
                  fill: ordinal('category'), stroke: '#fff' });
return v;

Penguin body-mass share per species — group and sum first, then tile by the aggregate:

const byMass = db.table('penguins').groupby(['species'], { total: sum('body_mass_g') });
const v = canvas(560, 400);
v.rect(byMass, { ...treemap('total').pick('x', 'y', 'w', 'h'),
                 fill: ordinal('species'), stroke: '#fff' });
return v;

Fitted labels — the text layer borrows each tile's solved box through a via and computes its midpoint in the same breath (f.x + f.w/2, f.y + f.h/2), then fitText sizes the label against that box. { shared: true } opts into one uniform size (capped at 14, no rotation):

const revenue = db.table('orders').rollup(
  db.table('products'), { left: 'product_id', right: 'id' },
  'category', { amount: sum({ sql: 'qty * price' }) });
const v = canvas(640, 460);
const rects = v.rect(revenue, { ...treemap('amount').pick('x', 'y', 'w', 'h'),
                                fill: ordinal('category'), stroke: '#fff' });
const center = { resolve: { cx: { fn: 'avg', arg: expr`f.x + f.w / 2` },
                            cy: { fn: 'avg', arg: expr`f.y + f.h / 2` } } };
v.text(revenue, {
  ...rects.via(eq('category', 'category'), center).pick({ x: 'cx', y: 'cy', w: 'w', h: 'h' }),
  ...fitText().pick('fontSize'),
  text: field('category'), anchor: 'middle', baseline: 'middle', fill: '#333',
});
return v;

Per-panel treemaps — because treemap is perCell, nesting it inside eqX panels gives each panel its own unit-square tiling of that panel's data. The revenue relation must keep year for the eq('year', 'year') nest to bind, so group by both keys (join then groupby(['year', 'category'])) rather than rolling up to category alone:

const revenue = db.table('orders')
  .join(db.table('products'), { left: 'product_id', right: 'id' })
  .groupby(['year', 'category'], { amount: sum({ sql: 'qty * price' }) });
const years = db.table('orders').groupby(['year'], {});
const v = canvas(720, 320);
v.rect(years, { ...eqX('year').pick('x', 'w'), y: 10, h: 300, fill: '#f5f6fa' })
 .rect(revenue,
   { ...treemap('amount').pick('x', 'y', 'w', 'h'), fill: ordinal('category'), stroke: '#fff' },
   { on: eq('year', 'year') });
return v;

See also

  • proportionalX / proportionalY — one-axis value partitions
  • force — the other dual-substrate space-partition layout (node placement)
  • eqX — panel splitter that treemap nests inside
  • fitText — label fitting used with the computed-center via above
